page.title: Ship
page.subtitle: Pull requests and deploys for projects with attached repos.
page.disabled_title: Ship Hub is off for this workspace
page.disabled_description: A workspace admin can turn on Ship Hub from Settings. Once enabled, GitHub pull requests and deploy state appear here, grouped by project.

empty.no_projects_description: Attach a GitHub repo to a project to see its pull requests and deploys here.
empty.no_token_description: Add a GitHub personal access token in Settings → Ship Hub. PRs from your project repos will appear here once it's set.

errors.token_expired_description: The token saved for this workspace was rejected by GitHub. Re-enter a fresh token to resume syncing.
errors.rate_limited_description: GitHub rate limit hit. Try again in about a minute.
errors.sync_failed_description: Couldn't fetch the latest pull requests. {{message}}

configure_dialog.description: Track which branch you deploy to which target. The token-based deploy doesn't run from here yet — these fields tell Ship Hub what to display when you log a deploy.
configure_dialog.auto_promote_hint: Phase 2 feature — visible here so the toggle is consistent across surfaces.

log_dialog.title: Log deploy
log_dialog.description: Record a deploy that already happened (Vercel CLI, GitHub Actions, manual ssh). Ship Hub doesn't trigger the deploy itself yet.
log_dialog.ref_hint: Defaults to the environment's target branch.

settings.enable_description: Surface GitHub pull requests and deploy state for projects with attached repos. When off, the sidebar entry hides and Ship Hub endpoints return 404.
settings.toast_toggle_failed: Failed to update Ship Hub setting
settings.token_description: Personal access token used to fetch PRs from project repositories. The token is stored encrypted and never returned to the UI.
